title: Illegal construction, locked terrace, multiple lapses: What made Delhi hotel fire rescue ops difficult
content: A Delhi bed and breakfast, operating illegally as a 25-room hotel despite a six-room license, flouted numerous safety norms. Flourish Stay lacked fire safety certificates, had a single exit, and its electronic gates and locked windows trapped guests during a devastating fire.  The probe revealed a shocking disregard for basic safety regulations.

title: 'Not suicide': Rahul blames 'broken system' after NEET aspirant's death; targets PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi slammed the Modi government over the NEET-UG paper leak, citing the tragic suicide of aspirant Akanksha. He highlighted the farmer father's sacrifices and blamed the "corrupt, broken system" for her death. Gandhi also questioned Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's continued tenure and accused PM Modi of damaging India's education.

title: How Ukraine brought the war to St Petersburg, Russia's strategic northern hub
content: As Russia hosted a significant economic forum, Ukrainian drones launched coordinated assaults on key energy and naval sites in Saint Petersburg. The incident, which left several injured, effectively confronted Moscow's narrative of resilience in the face of Western punitive measures. This operation showcases Ukraine's advancing long-range capabilities, taking aim at critical infrastructure deep within enemy territory.

title: Virat Kohli likely to miss Afghanistan ODIs; suspense continues over Rohit Sharma
content: Virat Kohli was in sublime form during IPL 2026, scoring 675 runs at an average of 56.25 and top-scoring with an unbeaten 75 in the title clash. He had reportedly felt discomfort in his right hamstring while batting during the final. Meanwhile, Rohit Sharma is yet to report to the BCCI's Centre of Excellence for a fitness assessment. With both players dealing with hamstring issues, the selectors could be forced to explore additional options ahead of the upcoming assignments.

title: Film producer and ex-CBFC chief Pahlaj Nihalani passes away due to liver cirrhosis
content: Veteran film producer and former CBFC chief Pahlaj Nihalani passed away on June 4, 2026, after a battle with liver cirrhosis. Known for backing early films of Govinda and Chunky Pandey, Nihalani produced hits like 'Aankhen' and 'Shola Aur Shabnam'. He chaired the CBFC from 2015 until his ousting in 2017. His final production, 'Rangeela Raja', released in 2019. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai sends a ‘thanks note’ to Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway
content: Alphabet has secured a massive $45 billion equity offering, with Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway leading with a $10 billion investment. This substantial capital infusion is earmarked for bolstering Google's AI compute infrastructure, a critical area identified by CEO Sundar Pichai. The company anticipates a total of $85 billion for this AI build-out, underscoring its strategic focus on artificial intelligence.

title: Microsoft AI CEO: My team is 'more concerned' about Anthropic, than Google, Meta and OpenAI
content: Microsoft's AI chief, Mustafa Suleyman, revealed a strategic shift, prioritizing competition with Anthropic over rivals like Google and Meta.  This focus stems from Anthropic's aggressive entry into enterprise software and coding tools, posing a direct threat to Microsoft's core business.  Microsoft is now developing its own advanced AI models to counter this challenge and reduce reliance on OpenAI.

title: On June 10, Modi will break Nehru’s record of longest elected PM stint
content: Narendra Modi is set to become the longest-serving unbroken head of government in India, surpassing Jawaharlal Nehru on June 10. Modi's tenure, beginning May 26, 2014, will reach 4,399 days, just ahead of Nehru's 4,398 days. This milestone highlights a significant shift in Indian political leadership and policy direction.

title: 10th straight defeat: PV Sindhu falls to An Se Young, crashes out of Indonesia Open
content: Sindhu, who had also gone down to the South Korean in the quarterfinals of last week's Singapore Open, lost 17-21, 14-21 in the round of 16 match.

title: $5 billion-plus company GitLab cuts hundreds of jobs, exits 22 countries; CEO blames it on AI
content: GitLab is laying off 14% of its workforce and exiting 22 countries as part of a restructuring to focus on the "agentic era" of software development. CEO Bill Staples stated that savings will be reinvested into AI products, particularly the Duo Agent Platform, to position GitLab as the enterprise platform for AI-driven software creation.

title: Ranveer's 'Don 3' row: Producers Guild to hold fresh talks as FWICE drops non-cooperation order
content: The Producers Guild of India is mediating the 'Don 3' dispute between Ranveer Singh and Excel Entertainment after FWICE withdrew its non-cooperation directive. The Guild aims for an amicable resolution and clearer industry protocols. Excel Entertainment has cleared pending payments despite reported losses, signaling a potential shift in Bollywood's professional agreements. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Tax cuts, easier bond rules on the cards: How India plans to attract more foreign investment
content: Reports suggest that PM Narendra Modi-led Union Cabinet on Wednesday cleared an ordinance that will amend the Income Tax Act and enable the proposed tax exemption.

title: ‘Everything works in Delhi’: Hotel owner blames ‘other person’ in Malviya Nagar fire probe
content: A devastating fire at a Malviya Nagar hotel, claiming 21 lives, has revealed alleged violations including operating far beyond capacity and lacking fire safety clearance. The owner, arrested, reportedly claimed ignorance of rules and delegated operations. Investigations are underway into structural changes and ownership of multiple properties.

title: Huge fireball, roof collapse: Kuwait shares video of Iranian drone strikes on airport- watch
content: Kuwait International Airport was struck by Iranian drones, causing significant damage to a passenger terminal and resulting in the death of an Indian national and injuries to dozens. Kuwait's defense ministry reported destroying numerous missiles and drones, while the Ministry of External Affairs condemned the attack and called for an immediate cessation of such violence.

title: 'I'm not running at all': Lalit Modi on the 'fugitive' tag and whether he'll return to India
content: Former IPL chairman Lalit Modi vehemently denies being a "fugitive," asserting he has never been convicted of any crime and labels the tag as media sensationalism. He argues that his global travel demonstrates he is not evading authorities, questioning the legal basis for any potential arrest after years of investigations.

title: Fresh NEET 2026 leak scare? NTA rushes Telegram 'paper sale' claims to CyberCrime
content: The National Testing Agency has addressed claims of NEET 2026 question papers being sold on Telegram. This follows the recent cancellation of the NEET UG 2026 examination due to alleged paper leaks. Millions of students were impacted by the cancellation. The Central Bureau of Investigation is now investigating the matter. The Supreme Court has also expressed concerns about examination security

title: 28L women get Rs 3,000 each under Bengal's Annapurna Yojana on day 1
content: Over 28 lakh women in the state have received Rs 3,000 each under the Annapurna Yojana through direct benefit transfer. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari launched the process, confirming that eligible applicants will receive the funds. The scheme aims to empower women, with applications open for three months.  Verification ensures government funds reach intended beneficiaries.

title: ‘No regrets’: Vijay Shankar on retirement, 2019 WC heartbreak and the ‘3D player’ tag
content: In an exclusive conversation with TimesofIndia.com following his retirement announcement, former India allrounder Vijay Shankar reflected on the emotional decision to step away, the injuries that altered his career, handling public criticism, his love for all-round cricket and why he still wants to continue playing overseas leagues.

title: Stunned, betrayed: TMC foot soldiers struggle with 31-day bad news cycle
content: Disbelief and anger grip Trinamool workers after a major electoral defeat, leading to internal turmoil and the dissolution of party committees. Loyalists express frustration over perceived organizational drift and the sidelining of old guards, while some blame external consultants and the induction of rival party leaders. Despite the shock, many remain committed to Mamata Banerjee, hoping for a party revival.

title: 'I was a passport agent, car broker before cricket': Krunal Pandya opens up
content: Five-time IPL champion Krunal Pandya's journey to success was forged in the crucible of early life struggles. Before cricket stardom, he navigated the world of paperwork, affidavits, and brokering second-hand cars, lessons that instilled resilience and a craving for tough challenges. These experiences shaped his approach to cricket, teaching him to embrace pressure and never give up.

title: NIA raids ex-TMC MLA's house in Bengal pre-poll bomb blast probe
content: The investigation was transferred to the NIA following demands for a central inquiry into the explosion. Since assuming charge of the case, the agency has arrested several people, including TMC leader Wahidul Islam. Officials from a special NIA team arrived at Molla's residence to gather information and examine possible links to the case. During the operation, Molla's wife and daughter were seen entering the premises.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chiefs eye Lions TE as Travis Kelce successor
content: The Kansas City Chiefs have been linked to a hypothetical trade for Detroit Lions tight end Sam LaPorta as a potential successor to Travis Kelce. The proposed deal would send a first- and third-round pick to Detroit for the 25-year-old Pro Bowler, who is entering the final year of his rookie contract. While LaPorta's recent injury history raises questions, his talent and long-term upside make him an intriguing target for Kansas City.

title: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini back in spotlight after explosive new claim
content: The Patriots' blockbuster acquisition of A.J. Brown quickly became overshadowed by controversy after a viral social media claim linked head coach Mike Vrabel to former NFL insider Dianna Russini. The allegation gained significant attention despite no evidence supporting it. Meanwhile, Brown's move from Philadelphia to New England has sparked optimism about the Patriots' offense, with former NFL star Chris Long believing the receiver can help maximize quarterback Drake Maye's development and accelerate the team's Super Bowl ambitions.

title: CBSE blocks 3.8 million cyberattack packets amid revaluation rush
content: CBSE said it successfully blocked a 3.8 million-packet denial-of-service (DoS) cyberattack targeting its verification and re-evaluation portal while maintaining uninterrupted services for students. Despite the attempted disruption, the portal processed over 56,000 applications for answer-book verification and re-evaluation. The Board said technical teams are enhancing performance and security. Partner banks, including SBI, Bank of Baroda, Indian Bank and Canara Bank, processed thousands of payment transactions as CBSE’s digital systems continue to face public scrutiny.

title: Lalit Modi finds boy who broke down after missing Virat Kohli autograph, promises signed IPL bat
content: A viral video of a young Virat Kohli fan, Yugveer Budhiraja, crying after failing to get an autograph deeply moved former IPL chairman Lalit Modi. Modi promised the boy a special bat signed by all players from the inaugural IPL season.

title: Maria Sharapova steals spotlight with stunning French Open return
content: Maria Sharapova made a surprise appearance at the 2025 French Open, delighting fans at Roland Garros. The five-time Grand Slam champion and two-time French Open winner posed for photos, met former star Gustavo Kuerten, and received praise across social media. Though retired since 2020, Sharapova remains one of tennis’ most popular figures and continues to attract worldwide attention.

title: Taylor Swift’s surprise wedding invite shocks fans, Blake Lively left out
content: Taylor Swift's upcoming wedding guest list is causing a stir. Karlie Kloss, with whom Swift had a public falling out, has received an invitation. However, Blake Lively, a former close friend, has not been invited. This decision stems from Lively's legal issues that exposed Swift's private information. Swift is prioritizing a drama-free event, showcasing her careful selection of confidantes.

title: 4 killed as massive fire rips through hospital in Bihar's Muzaffarpur
content: Tragedy struck Prasad Hospital in Muzaffarpur, Bihar, as a devastating fire engulfed the ICU on Thursday. District Magistrate Subrat Kumar Sen confirmed that several lives were lost in the blaze. Firefighting teams responded swiftly, bringing the inferno under control. Initial reports indicate three fatalities, with more details anticipated as the situation unfolds.

title: Delhi hotel fire: Family booked rooms to stay with ailing elder; 8 of them are dead
content: A family gathering to be with a gravely ill elder turned tragic when eight members died in a hotel fire. The Aggarwal family, who had traveled to be with 75-year-old Radheshyam, were among the victims. Relatives are devastated and have not yet informed the ailing patriarch of the catastrophe.

title: Pakistan court sentences 2 men to death for raping French woman before her kids
content: The incident led to widespread protests, with demonstrators demanding justice for the victim and criticising remarks by Punjab police officials that were widely seen as blaming her for the attack. Protesters were calling for the sacking of lead police investigator Omar Sheikh, who had blamed the victim for the incident, saying that "our society does not permit women to be out late at night alone".

title: 'Where do we bowl?': SRH star recalls Vaibhav Sooryavanshi nightmare
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi delivered a record-shattering IPL 2026 campaign, highlighted by a 97 off 29 balls in the Eliminator. His sensational batting, including 72 sixes, earned him the Orange Cap and MVP award. Sooryavanshi's adaptability impressed opponents, with Liam Livingstone noting his rapid development against various bowling tactics.

title: Israel, Lebanon agree to renew ceasefire; create Lebanese security zones
content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to renew a ceasefire and establish security zones in Lebanon, barring Hezbollah from these areas. This US-mediated deal hinges on a complete halt to Hezbollah fire and their withdrawal south of the Litani River, with the Lebanese army taking control.

title: 'Lack of effort' to free Imran Khan? PTI lawmakers revolt against party leadership
content: A significant internal rift has emerged within PTI in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa as lawmakers express dissatisfaction with the party leadership's efforts to secure Imran Khan's release. Disgruntled MPAs, reportedly denied cabinet positions, have penned a letter to the PTI chairman, arguing the release campaign has lost momentum and become ineffective.

title: Trump's 12.5% tariff move under Section 301: What it means for India, trade talks
content: The Section 301 probe launched by the US in March 2026 is a known variable in India’s trade deal talks with America. Yet, the proposal to impose duties on around 60 countries assumes significance at a time when a delegation from the US is in India to finalise terms of the India-US trade deal.

title: Ropes, mattresses, bricks, bare hands: How locals fought to save guests in South Delhi hotel fire
content: Local heroes emerged as a fire engulfed a New Delhi bed and breakfast. Traders, neighbours, and labourers bravely used bricks, ropes, and mattresses to rescue trapped occupants. They smashed windows, created escape routes from rooftops, and entered the burning building to carry out unconscious victims, demonstrating extraordinary courage and community spirit.

title: DKS is Karnataka captain, but Siddaramaiah’s XI dominate team
content: D.K. Shivakumar sworn in as Karnataka's 24th CM, with G. Parameshwara as Deputy CM. The new cabinet balances caste and regional interests, reflecting Siddaramaiah's influence. Early initiatives focus on youth welfare, including free student bus passes and a private job portal. The ceremony highlighted religious traditions and drew national Congress leaders.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Official Album Released: Full 18-Track Lineup Revealed
content: FIFA has unveiled the 18-track Official FIFA World Cup 2026 Album, the largest music project ever linked to the tournament. Featuring global stars such as LISA, Shakira, Future, Tyla, The Rolling Stones and Burna Boy, the album aims to unite fans through music and football. Several songs are already available, while many artists will perform during Countdown Concerts and opening ceremonies across Mexico, Canada and the United States. The project also forms part of FIFA’s wider entertainment strategy ahead of the first-ever World Cup final halftime show.

title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: Southwest monsoon arrives in Kerala, says IMD
content: Heavy rains hit parts of Kerala; India Meteorological Department issues orange alert for Alappuzha, Kottayam, and Ernakulam for three hours

title: Supreme Court dismisses plea seeking guidelines for time-bound disposal of cases
content: The plea also sought formulation and implementation of a uniform national case flow management policy applicable to all courts in the country

title: Monsoon ‘likely to set in’ over Kerala around June 4: IMD
content: Upper-air cyclonic circulation off the south Kerala coast is expected to aid final push, and the IMD has forecast isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over Kerala over the next six to seven days

title: Kerala monsoon onset: Why southwest monsoon arrival matters for India
content: The southwest monsoon onset in Kerala not only impacts local weather patterns but also plays a crucial role in shaping India's food security, agricultural GDP, and water supply across the nation.

title: TVK allots lone Rajya Sabha seat from Tamil Nadu to Congress for bypoll
content: With this, the Congress’ representation from Tamil Nadu in the Rajya Sabha is likely to increase to three, the highest in recent decades

title: Hard disk theft at TNPDCL: Data backup initiated, probe under way, says Tamil Nadu Minister Nirmalkumar
content: Speaking to journalists at the Secretariat, Mr. Nirmalkumar said several hard disks had been reported missing from the TNPDCL headquarters last month

title: WFI plea against HC order allowing wrestler Vinesh Phogat for selection trials infructuous: SC
content: A Bench of Justices P.S. Narasimha and Aravind Kumar, which refused to go into the observations made by the high court, said this court should not be taken as having reiterated the findings and observations made in the order

title: Kerala’s Plus One admissions see 4.44 lakh applications
content: Maximum number of applicants from Malappuram – 82,678. Least from Idukki – 11,420. Wayanad has 11,790 applicants

title: Absolute incompetence of BJP unmasked by NFHS-6 data: Kharge
content: Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge accused the BJP of having a five-step formula to hide its sin: burying selected data, abandoning the vulnerable, advertising “Sabka Saath” and “Amrit Kaal”, manipulating the narrative and protecting Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s PR at all costs

title: Naidu asks ONGC to speed up K-G basin projects and use CSR for villages
content: ONGC chairman Arun Kumar Singh calls on Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu, who asks the firm to speed up its Krishna-Godavari basin projects and spend CSR funds on nearby villages

title: Nepal is a priority partner, says Modi after meeting RSP chief
content: By focusing on shared civilizational bonds, digital corridors, and seamless connectivity, we can truly build a partnership defined by progress and mutual trust, says Rabi Lamichanne

title: Is shoot-to-disable the new normal? | Explained
content: Why is Operation Langda a pragmatic strategy? Has a sporadic enforcement tactic become a routine practice? What makes it legally defensible ?To what extent does the State endorse half-encounters? Is it a self-sustaining system? Why are piecemeal reforms insufficient?

title: Kerala faces burden of ₹5.07-lakh crore liabilities; fiscal structure under ‘serious’ strain, says White Paper tabled by CM
content: Kerala faces a ‘large burden’ of outstanding liabilities (₹5.07 lakh crore), committed expenditures (77% of total revenue receipts - TRR), and interest payments (20.9% of TRR), notes the document titled ‘Kerala’s Fiscal Health: A Status Report’

title: BCs are 56% of A.P., but hold only 40 MLA seats, says former DGP Purnachandra Rao
content: There is a serious mismatch between population strength and political representation in Andhra Pradesh, says Purnachandra Rao

title: Vizag set to host A.P.’s first India Semiconductor Mission project
content: Groundbreaking ceremony for Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test facility proposed at Tarluvada is scheduled for June 8; the facility envisages investment of ₹2,387.81 crore and is expected to generate 1,000 jobs;

title: Explained | When an economy built on cheap labour faces higher wages
content: The evidence showing limited or no job loss challenges the idea that cheap labour is a necessity for employment

title: Demography panel may study SIR exclusions; seeks details from government
content: Panel to recommend “well-organised and permanent operational system for the legal, fair and time bound identification, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants residing in the country”

title: Census fieldwork contradicts government data, enumerators asked to revisit, review data
content: Director Census Operations (DCO), Rajasthan, writes to all district functionaries that ‘during the analysis of field data collected so far, some discrepancies have been noticed’;  enumerators allege they have been asked not to ‘select options that may show the government in poor light’

title: The Hindu Huddle on Campus: ‘Social media protects people from being held accountable for actions’
content: Moderator Vishu Dev C.H., a faculty member from Amity School for Communication Enhancement and Transformation, pointed to concerns around anxiety, depression, distractions, and mental health and well-being

title: Google AI lab inaugurated at Vignan’s University in A.P.
content: Students and faculty will gain access to Gemini Enterprise for Education, providing hands-on experience in Artificial Intelligence, Generative AI and Agentic AI technologies

title: Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan tables White Paper on State finances in Assembly
content: K.N. Balagopal, who was Finance Minister in previous CPI(M)-led LDF government, registers dissent arguing that the document should have been drafted by State Finance department and not by an external committee

title: NIA searches former TMC MLA Saukat Mollah's house in Bengal in Bhangar blast case
content: The agency also searched Saukat Mollah's office as part of the operation. Saukat Mollah's wife and daughter reached the residence while the searches were underway

title: Two associates of foreign-based terror operative held from Punjab's Mohali
content: The operation led to the recovery of an Improvised Explosive Device (IED), resulting in the foiling of a plot aimed at targeting critical public infrastructure in SAS Nagar (Mohali), police said

title: White Paper on Kerala finances calls for KIIFB overhaul, forensic audit of accounts
content: The document, however, notes it will be “wasteful” to dismantle KIIFB framework which has built valuable capabilities over the years

title: 4 dead, others injured in Muzaffarpur hospital fire in Bihar
content: Though the cause of the incident was yet to be ascertained, the primary reports suggest that a short circuit was the reason for the fire

title: D.K. Shivakumar takes oath in the name of Ajjayya, and with the Constitution in hand
content: D.K. Shivakumar took oath in the name of the late seer Veera Gangadhara Ajjayya, his spiritual mentor, while holding the Constitution

title: Cyberabad mulls paid parking on Knowledge City roads amid traffic, street racing concerns
content: Active towing, designated short-stay parking zones proposed on key Raidurgam and Madhapur IT corridor stretches.

title: Monsoon 2026: How to track clouds with IMD’s INSAT imagery?
content: The monsoon is not just about the rain that falls to the ground. It is also important to track the storms that are formed kilometers above ground in the atmosphere.

title: I will not take unilateral decisions and will take everyone along, says DKS
content: His statement is being seen as an effort to win over his party colleagues, particularly his political detractors, who are concerned that he may bulldoze them with his aggressive functioning style

title: Realtor accuses live-in partner of taking ₹1 crore, marrying someone else in Bengaluru
content: He claims to have given her nearly ₹1 crore, and also handed over around 500 grams of gold, during the course of their nearly 8-year relationship

title: Chinnaswamy stadium stampede: One year on, grief still fresh for victims’ kin
content: Family members of the victims of the Chinnaswamy stadium stampede term it a dark day and say they never want such an incident to recur

title: Bengaluru stampede: A sea of cheers drowned out by wailing sirens
content: For fans of RCB in Bengaluru, an 18-year wait to celebrate the sweet triumph of their favourite team turned into a bitter tragedy as a crowd surge led to a stampede on June 4, 2025. This is The Hindu’s complete coverage of the sequence of events that led to the mishap and its aftermath.

title: Loyalists rally behind Mamata, say an ousted leader cannot lead Trinamool in Assembly
content: Earlier in the day, the Trinamool had dissolved all its party committees, including district and block units; party leaders say ‘Mamata is the original Trinamool Congress’

title: Delhi hotel fire: Locals turn saviours amid wails, screams, burning bodies
content: Rescuers say building’s single entry-exit point and lack of accessible windows made the rescue operation difficult

title: Monsoon may remain subdued after vigorous onset in Kerala
content: The southwest monsoon is set to reach the Kerala coast on June 4. IMD issues orange alert for Alappuzha, Kottayam, Ernakulam, and Thrissur on Wednesday, warning of intense rainfall

title: “Telangana is the Jagir of its four crore people”, KTR tells Pawan Kalyan
content: “No one stopped Mr. Pawan Kalyan from contesting and he has already done it twice here. But still he is yet to digest Telangana’s identity itself,” BRS working president Rama Rao charged

title: Assam, Meghalaya farmers resume farming in disputed border area after weeks of conflict
content: The Tapat-Lapangap area is one of six inter-State border sectors where a 54-year-old territorial dispute remains to be resolved

title: Indian national killed in attack on Kuwait airport
content: In a post on X, the Embassy of India in Kuwait expressed its “deepest condolences at the tragic demise of an Indian national due to an attack on the airport in Kuwait today”

title: Cockroach Janta Party zeroes in on Pradhan resignation demand, sets protest for June 6
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke will come from the U.S. to seek permission for Jantar Mantar protest; CJP calls on people across the political spectrum to join protest without party or organisational banners

title: No place for third parties in border issues: MEA in response to Nepal PM while hosting country’s ruling party chief
content: MEA spokesperson responds to claims made by Balen Shah who had asserted that the boundary issue is a complex one with both India and Nepal having possession of each other’s territory and had sought intervention of the U.K. and China

title: Stage set for D.K. Shivakumar to take over as new CM of Karnataka
content: About 10 to 15 Ministers likely to be sworn in on Wednesday; no clarity on Deputy Chief Minister posts

title: D.K. Shivakumar sworn-in as 25th Chief Minister of Karnataka
content: Mr. Shivakumar, who took over the reigns of the State from veteran OBC leader Siddaramaiah, was sworn in along with 13 Cabinet ministers including Dr. G. Parameshwara.

title: Remove restrictions on visitors to Secretariat: CPI(M)
content: A lot of restrictions are already in place. The new measures will prevent people from approaching the government machinery, says P. Shanmugam, State Secretary of the CPI(M), in a post on social media

title: 36 arrests in a day as Kochi City police launch ‘Operation Toofan’
content: Kochi City police registered 33 NDPS cases during raids across 90 locations as part of the Statewide anti-narcotics drive. Seven COTPA cases booked

title: Opinion: Opinion | Has Mamata Banerjee Reached Her Political Endgame?
content: The coming weeks will decide whether the TMC survives as a coherent force or fragments into irrelevance, a fate that would redefine not just Bengal but opposition dynamics across the entire nation.

title: Caste, Clout, Continuity: Congress Ticks All Boxes In Karnataka, Except One
content: Chief Minister DK Shivakumar and State Revenue Minister Krishna Byre Gowda provide representation to the influential Vokkaliga community, which remains politically dominant across the Old Mysuru region.

title: Narrow Escape For Madhya Pradesh MLA After SUV Hits Wall Trying To Save Cow
content: Madhya Pradesh MLA Yogendra Singh Baba survives SUV accident after driver loses control avoiding a cow near Banjari village.

title: Arunachal Christian Forum Demands Suspension Of State Religion Act Rules
content: The Arunachal Christian Forum demands suspension of APFRA rules framing over religious freedom concerns.

title: Weather Live Updates: Southwest Monsoon Arrives In Kerala
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over Kerala during the next week.

title: S Jaishankar Meets Venezuela's Acting President, Discusses Bilateral Ties
content: New Delhi, June 4 (IANS) External Affairs Minister (EAM) S. Jaishankar on Thursday called on Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodriguez in New Delhi, appreciating her sustained commitment to strengthening the partnership between India and Venezuela

title: From The Sahara's Sands, A New And Dangerous Order Is Taking Shape Fast
content: The Malian Defence Minister has been assassinated, separatists have 'partnered' with Al Qaeda-linked groups, and a military junta, backed by Russian forces, is working overtime to douse the fires.

title: Scandal Hits D-Street? How Rajesh Exports 'Faked' Rs 15 Lakh Crore Revenue
content: Rajesh Exports 'Scam': SEBI alleged that the revenues reported at the group level were higher than the revenues that could actually be verified.

title: Ohio University Reaches $100 Million Settlement With 280 Sex Abuse Victims
content: The settlement with 279 of the 280 former students was ratified by the university's board on Wednesday. It followed years of litigation over accusations of decades of abuse by Richard Strauss.

title: Nepali Climber Missing On Everest For 6 Days Found Alive
content: The experienced Hillary Dawa Sherpa vanished on the upper reaches of the world's highest mountain early on May 30.

title: Donald Trump Defines Iran 'Ceasefire' As "Shooting In Moderate Manner"
content: The American commander-in-chief also implied that it was possible that the US and Iran could come together for a peace deal "over the weekend".

title: Maharashtra Teen Rams Speeding Car Into Pedestrians, Vehicles, 5 Injured
content: According to reports, chaos erupted on Ramanandnagar Road after the incident, with enraged locals gathering at the spot.

title: Uttarakhand MBA Student Seen On Camera Hours Before Going Missing During Trek
content: Babita Pandey, a 24-year-old MBA student, went missing during a trek to Dayara Bugyal in Uttarakhand's Uttarkashi six days ago.

title: Massive Fire At Chennai Dumpyard, Thick Smoke Engulfs Surrounding Areas
content: A fleet of fire tenders was rushed to the spot as firefighters battled the flames. According to officials, six fire tenders were deployed immediately to prevent the fire from spreading further.

title: Amit Shah To Chair 73rd North Eastern Council Session In Shillong Today
content: Amit Shah will chair 73rd Plenary Session of North Eastern Council session in Shillong and reviews border security in Tripura.

title: Delhi Hotel Owner Drove Past Burning Building, Didn't Stop 'Out Of Fear'
content: Lavkesh Bajaj, owner of Flourish Stay B&B said he drove past his burning hotel instead of stopping by to help people rescue those trapped in the fire.

title: Kerala To Table White Paper On Finances Today, May Trigger Political Row
content: The White Paper is expected to outline the state's current financial position, including details of debt, revenue, and alleged financial leakages.

title: Food Contamination Causes 1.5 Million Deaths Annually: WHO
content: After analysing 194 countries between 2000 and 2021, the United Nations health agency found that 886 million people contract an illness linked to the consumption of unsafe food a year

title: US charges tech CEO over alleged exports of restricted equipment to Iran
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Princess Diana’s parenting lessons on deep, empathetic communication with children
content: While not jumping with joy seeing every dog that comes her way (to the bewilderment of the dog owner and the dog), Haimanti fantasizes about fantasy books or classics to read and re-read. That could be the gist of it all, except for the aroma of biryani that beckons; or that idea that's stuck in the head and refuses to go till it's penned down. Read More

title: Experiment aborted too soon? How 'sidelined' Annamalai drifted away from BJP in Tamil Nadu
content: the former IPS officer has quit the BJP . he joined BJP in 2020 and made the party visible in the state like never before . sources close to Annamalai claim he has moved on and wants to put his own plans into action .

title: ED summons Abhishek Banerjee in teacher recruitment case; residence security removed
content: abhishek Banerjee was asked to appear before ED’s Kolkata zonal office on June 15 . the ED launched a probe in the primary teachers’ recruitment case based on an FIR registered by the CBI against alleged irregularities in teachers’ appointments . officers removed the police kiosks, barricades, scanner machines and other surveillance equipment .
